level_signals: 5+ years of technical program management experience
Drives cross-org decisions across 10+ cross-functional teams
Newly created, high-impact role described as 'strategically consequential'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of technical program management experience with demonstrated success in driving complex cross-organizational programs.
Strong product sense and ability to understand enterprise customer needs

Demonstrated ability to communicate with precision and credibility across engineering, product, and executive audiences, translating complex technical trade-offs into strategic decisions, and driving alignment.
Demonstrated ability to lead and align cross-functional teams — driving decisions, resolving conflict, and building consensus across organizations with competing priorities.
Proven track record of successful program delivery in complex technical environments
Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in product management or product operations
Experience with developer platforms, API products, or payment systems

scope.surface: Extensibility platform — Scripts, Custom Objects, and Workflows primitives within the DEeP organization
primary_metrics_inferred: Total addressable market expansion via extensibility
Program health and on-time delivery across workstreams
core_responsibilities: Drive cross-org decisions across DRIs in Script Platform, Custom Objects, Apps, SDK, Observability, DevEx, and Versioning
Own the critical path to major launches — proactively shaping scope, driving descope decisions, and negotiating trade-offs across teams
Influence and shape product and engineering roadmaps — bringing a program-level perspective to team planning cycles
Support ProServ and Partnerships — ensure ProServ and Partnerships requirements surface in the roadmap
Set the program strategy in partnership with DEeP leadership

green_flags: Newly created, high-impact role described as 'strategically consequential' — clear mandate and organizational investment
Well-defined scope across three named extensibility primitives (Scripts, Custom Objects, Workflows)
Explicitly states preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement — encourages broad applicant pool
Cross-functional ownership across 10+ teams signals significant organizational visibility
Flagship product launch tied to this program (Workflows graduating to GA)
red_flags: No location, remote eligibility, or work authorization information provided in the JD
Role is newly created — may lack established processes or clear organizational precedents
Very broad cross-org scope (Scripts, Custom Objects, Workflows + ProServ + Partnerships) could indicate diffuse accountability
